page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at 2.2 GHz. The system features 170.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.08 GB per core, and includes two dedicated NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a total of 80 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates that single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25%, as demonstrated by stress-ng, gzip compression, and Geekbench single-core tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the middle 50% average tier. Conversely, the server demonstrates top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference tasks, including prompt processing and text generation across 7B and 70B parameter models.

From a resource density perspective, the high ratio of GPU acceleration and memory relative to the CPU allocation makes this server a specialized option where the primary cost driver is the accelerator hardware. It is designed for workloads that offload processing to the GPUs rather than relying on raw single-core CPU speed. Appropriate workloads include LLM inference, machine learning training, and GPU-accelerated data science tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores from an Intel Xeon processor, and 170.0 GB of system memory. The hardware configuration is distinguished by two integrated NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80 GB of total VRAM, while local storage is absent. Performance benchmarks show a clear contrast: single-core CPU execution is in the bottom 25%, and multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth operations rank in the middle 50%. However, the server achieves top-tier results in the top 10% for large language model inference, including prompt processing and text generation for models up to 70B parameters. This profile makes the server highly efficient for GPU-bound workloads such as deep learning inference and parallel computing tasks, rather than CPU-heavy sequential processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 24 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80GB VRAM, delivering top-tier performance for large language model inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g features 24 vCPUs, 170GB RAM, and two NVIDIA A100 GPUs, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized architecture featuring dual graphics processors and high memory capacity for demanding parallel computing and language model inference.

Features 24 vCPUs and 170.0 GB of system memory with dedicated Intel Xeon allocation
Delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model inference
